qa_pairs.question: How do you upload a file to an Arbutus object storage bucket using the `s3cmd` tool?
qa_pairs.answer: To upload a file to an Arbutus object storage bucket, use the `s3cmd put` command. For example, `$ s3cmd put --guess-mime-type FILE_NAME.dat s3://BUCKET_NAME/FILE_NAME.dat`. The `--guess-mime-type` parameter helps in automatically determining the file's Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) type based on its extension, otherwise it defaults to `binary/octet-stream`.

qa_pairs.question: What `s3cmd` command is used to delete a specific file from an Arbutus object storage bucket?
qa_pairs.answer: To delete a specific file from an Arbutus object storage bucket, use the `s3cmd rm` command. The syntax is `$ s3cmd rm s3://BUCKET_NAME/FILE_NAME.dat`, where you specify the bucket name and the exact file to be deleted.

qa_pairs.question: How can you make an Arbutus object storage bucket and all its contents publicly accessible using `s3cmd`?
qa_pairs.answer: To make an Arbutus object storage bucket and recursively all its files publicly accessible, use the `s3cmd setacl --acl-public -r s3://BUCKET_NAME/` command. After this, files within the bucket can be accessed via URLs such as `https://object-arbutus.alliancecan.ca/PROJECT_ID:BUCKET_NAME/FILE_NAME.dat`.
